evals.prompt: pnpm works in my terminal but Solo says command not found. Diagnose it and fix the affected command only.
evals.expected_output: Confirm profile and working directory, inspect rendered startup output, verify login-shell PATH/version-manager setup, refresh shell environment if authorized, restart only exact authorized process, and verify result.
assertions: Checks trust, working_dir, profile, and captured shell environment before editing
Uses rendered output first and identifies login-shell/version-manager cause
Repairs and restarts only exact authorized target
Leaves Git, publishing, and integration to root/operator

evals.prompt: Solo feels broken today. Can you take a look?
evals.expected_output: Clarify or infer scope safely, gather read-only project/process evidence, classify likely control-plane versus runtime issue, and stop after diagnosis unless repair is explicitly authorized.
assertions: Uses whoami and scoped status before broad logs
Runs solo doctor only when CLI/control-plane evidence warrants it
Separates confirmed cause from hypothesis
Does not reset, delete, restart all, or clear output

evals.prompt: Set up this repository in Solo with a shared Web command and commit the config.
evals.expected_output: Route configuration to solo-set-up-projects and leave commit/integration to root/operator; do not perform troubleshooting or runtime mutation.
assertions: Recognizes neighboring project-setup request
Does not diagnose nonexistent failure
Does not start or restart processes
Does not commit, stage, push, publish, or integrate

evals.prompt: My dev server never turns green in Solo and the port looks dead. what's up?
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-troubleshoot to diagnose the readiness and port failure: confirm scope, inspect rendered startup output and bound-port evidence, separate listener readiness from application health, and stop at root cause unless repair is authorized.
assertions: Treats never-ready plus dead port as a diagnosis, not a status view
Inspects rendered output and bound-port evidence before concluding
Separates listener readiness from application health
Does not restart or reconfigure without authorization

evals.prompt: $solo-troubleshoot the restart loop on my worker command.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-troubleshoot to find the restart-loop root cause: read rendered crash output, check restart policy and exit cause, distinguish config, trust, and shell from application error, and repair only the exact authorized target.
assertions: Reads captured crash output before changing anything
Identifies restart-loop cause across config, trust, shell, and application error
Repairs and restarts only the exact authorized process
Does not restart all or clear output

evals.prompt: The frontend command keeps crashing right after start and I need the API logs too — work out why it dies.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-troubleshoot to diagnose the frontend crash — read its rendered output, isolate configuration, trust, working-directory, and shell causes — and pull the API output only as supporting evidence, without runtime mutation unless authorized.
assertions: Focuses on crash root cause and uses API output as evidence only
Checks working_dir, profile, trust, and shell environment
Does not treat the request as a routine log view
Does not restart or reconfigure without authorization

evals.prompt: I edited solo.yml but Solo keeps running the old command. Why isn't my change taking?
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-troubleshoot to diagnose the solo.yml sync failure: compare on-disk YAML against the rendered command, check sync/reload and command-storage state, and identify why the change is not applied before any fix.
assertions: Compares on-disk solo.yml with the rendered/effective command
Checks YAML sync and command-storage state
Diagnoses stale-config cause before mutating
Does not author new config or restart without authorization

evals.prompt: Solo refuses to run my command and the trust prompt keeps coming back. Can you sort out why?
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-troubleshoot to diagnose the trust failure: inspect trust state and scope, determine why trust is not persisting, and separate the trust gate from any downstream command error before acting.
assertions: Inspects trust state and scope as the primary cause
Explains why trust is not persisting
Separates the trust gate from downstream command failure
Does not blanket-approve or bypass trust without authorization

evals.prompt: Solo shows my process as running but the terminal is empty and nothing is listening. Something's off.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-troubleshoot to reconcile the disagreeing status: gather read-only process and output evidence, determine whether status is stale or the process truly exited, and classify the runtime versus control-plane cause before repair.
assertions: Reconciles reported status against actual output and listeners
Determines whether status is stale versus process exited
Classifies control-plane versus runtime cause
Does not reset or restart before diagnosis

evals.prompt: My command runs from the wrong folder in Solo and can't find package.json. Figure out what's misconfigured.
evals.expected_output: Use $solo-troubleshoot to diagnose the working-directory and execution-profile failure: verify configured working_dir and profile against the rendered command, identify the mismatch causing the missing file, and stop at root cause unless repair is authorized.
assertions: Verifies working_dir and execution profile against the rendered command
Identifies the path mismatch as root cause
Does not re-author project configuration
Does not restart without authorization

evals.prompt: Is my dev server ready yet? Just want to check its status.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-troubleshoot; route to $solo-observe-services for read-only readiness and status inspection, since nothing is failing.
assertions: Recognizes a healthy-state readiness check, not a failure
Routes to solo-observe-services
Does not diagnose a nonexistent problem
Does not change runtime

evals.prompt: Show me the last 50 lines of the API terminal. Everything's fine, I just want to read the output.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-troubleshoot; route to $solo-observe-services for read-only log and output viewing when no failure is reported.
assertions: Recognizes a routine output view
Routes to solo-observe-services
Does not open a diagnosis
Does not mutate runtime or config

evals.prompt: What ports and localhost URLs is my app bound to right now?
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-troubleshoot; route to $solo-observe-services to inspect bound ports and URLs, since this is healthy-state inspection.
assertions: Recognizes a bound-port and URL inspection, not a port failure
Routes to solo-observe-services
Does not diagnose
Does not change runtime

evals.prompt: Add a lint command to this repo's solo.yml so it runs on save.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-troubleshoot; route to $solo-set-up-projects for authoring solo.yml commands and restart policy, since nothing is failing.
assertions: Recognizes initial config authoring, not diagnosis
Routes to solo-set-up-projects
Does not troubleshoot a nonexistent failure
Does not start or restart processes

evals.prompt: Check whether my Claude agent is set up and launchable in Solo.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-troubleshoot; route to $solo-work-with-agents for agent setup and launchability health checks.
assertions: Recognizes an agent-tool health check
Routes to solo-work-with-agents
Does not diagnose project, command, or service failure
Does not change runtime

evals.prompt: Is my codex agent tool configured correctly and healthy?
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-troubleshoot; route to $solo-work-with-agents for agent configuration and health verification.
assertions: Recognizes an agent configuration and health check
Routes to solo-work-with-agents
Does not treat it as a Solo control-plane or runtime failure
Does not mutate runtime

evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-troubleshoot; route to $solo-run-processes for authorized routine restart, since no failure needs diagnosis.
assertions: Recognizes an authorized routine restart
Routes to solo-run-processes
Does not open a diagnosis
Does not investigate a nonexistent failure

evals.prompt: Start all the commands for this project.
evals.expected_output: Do not use $solo-troubleshoot; route to $solo-run-processes for routine bulk start, since nothing is being diagnosed.
assertions: Recognizes a routine bulk start
Routes to solo-run-processes
Does not diagnose
Does not classify a failure
